things.name: Old Town (Glowne Miasto)
things.description: Magnificently restored Hanseatic old town with the Royal Road and merchant houses

things.name: Westerplatte
things.description: Where WWII began on 1 September 1939 - monument and museum on the peninsula

things.name: European Solidarity Centre
things.description: Museum and library dedicated to the Solidarity movement that ended Communism in Poland

things.name: St Mary's Church
things.description: One of the world's largest Gothic brick churches with 25 chapels

things.name: Long Market (Dlugi Targ)
things.description: Elegant main street with the Neptune Fountain and Golden Gate

things.name: Amber Museum
things.description: World's finest amber collection in the Gothic Great Mill

things.name: Gdansk Crane (Zuraw)
things.description: Massive 15th-century medieval harbour crane, symbol of Gdansk's mercantile past

things.name: Artus Court
things.description: Gothic merchants' meeting house with the world's tallest Renaissance tiled stove

things.name: Museum of World War II
things.description: One of the world's most comprehensive WWII museums opened in 2017

things.name: Sopot Beach & Pier
things.description: Europe's longest wooden pier and Poland's most famous Baltic Sea resort
